justification: All pass criteria met: 10 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria covering happy path (AC-1,2,3), already-shipped/delivered rejection (AC-4,5,6), already-cancelled idempotency (AC-8), authorization (AC-7), terminal state (AC-9), and audit (AC-10). Multiple ambiguities surfaced in both low_confidence_areas and escalations (cancellable statuses, refund policy, partial cancellation, who can cancel, time windows). Confidence level is 40, well below 65 threshold. Invented rules (cancellation fee, refund tiers) are explicitly flagged as assumptions, not presented as facts. Edge cases section is thoroughly populated with 8 scenarios.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough: 10 acceptance criteria far exceed the minimum 3, covering happy paths across multiple statuses plus edge cases
Confidence level of 40 appropriately reflects the underspecified single-sentence input
Three well-structured escalations with options and recommendations, clearly flagging cancellable statuses, refund policy, and partial cancellation as unknowns
All invented business rules (cancellation fee, refund tiers, cancellable status set) are explicitly called out as assumptions in the confidence section
Edge cases section is rich — includes race conditions, payment gateway failure, idempotency on retry, and partial order cancellation boundary
Who-can-cancel ambiguity is addressed via BR-2 and AC-7, plus the escalation on operator-initiated cancellations
Time window restriction not explicitly surfaced as an ambiguity — no mention of calendar/clock-based cancellation deadlines (e.g., 'within 30 minutes of placement')
Ubiquitous language section adds useful domain framing with clear definitions for cancellation-specific terms

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: 10 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria covering happy path (AC-1, AC-2), shipped/delivered rejection (AC-4, AC-5), already-cancelled idempotency (AC-6), plus authorization, audit, and edge cases. 6 explicit low_confidence_areas surfaced including cancellation window boundaries, fee structure, partial cancellation scope, and courier compensation. 5 escalations raised including who can cancel (self-service vs agents). confidence.level=45, well under 65 threshold. Business rules like cancellation fees are clearly flagged as assumptions, not presented as facts. edge_cases section is extensively populated with 8 scenarios.

observations.text: 10 acceptance criteria far exceed the minimum 3, covering happy path, multiple rejection states, authorization, audit trail, courier notification, and refund failure
confidence.level=45 appropriately reflects the underspecified single-sentence input
All three required ambiguities surfaced: cancellation window (escalation #1), time/fee restrictions (escalation #2), and who can cancel (escalation #5)
Assumptions section explicitly lists inferred rules (e.g., cancellation fees, linear status progression) as assumptions rather than facts
8 edge cases covering race conditions, idempotency, promo handling, partial cancellation, and payment edge cases

Bird invented detailed business rules (BR-1 through BR-8) with specific status-based fee tiers that go beyond flagging unknowns — while marked as assumptions, the level of specificity (e.g., 'Dispatched but not Picked Up may be cancelled with fee') could be mistaken for confirmed requirements
Each invented rule includes invariant_justification explaining whether it is a hard constraint or changeable policy, helping readers distinguish facts from assumptions

trials.justification: Meets all pass criteria: 10 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria covering happy path (AC-1), already-shipped/delivered (AC-5), already-cancelled idempotency (AC-7), and many more edge cases. Multiple explicit ambiguities surfaced in both escalations and low_confidence_areas — cancellation window unspecified, fee structure undefined, who can cancel unclear. confidence.level=45, well under 65. Business rules are clearly marked as assumptions with invariant_justification explaining policy vs physical constraints. Edge cases section is extensively populated with 9 scenarios.

observations.text: 10 acceptance criteria far exceed the minimum 3, covering happy path, multiple edge cases, authorization, idempotency, and audit trail
confidence.level=45 correctly reflects the underspecified input
4 explicit escalations surfaced: cancellation window undefined, fee structure undefined, who 'the user' is, and missing codebase access
low_confidence_areas lists 6 specific unknowns including exact statuses, fee amounts, refund policy, and promo handling
Business rules like BR-2/BR-3 (fee structure) are explicitly marked invariant=false with justification that these are policy choices, not presented as facts
Assumptions section clearly lists all inferred domain knowledge, distinguishing it from confirmed requirements
9 edge cases covering race conditions, payment failures, physical package handling, idempotency, and multi-package scenarios

Bird invented fairly detailed business rules (cancellation fees, partial refunds, InTransit blocking) — though these are flagged as assumptions and marked non-invariant, they go beyond what the spec states. This is borderline but acceptable since they are clearly caveated.
